MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Wismer & Becker Contracting Engineers
Dustin M. Seesemann of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Arthur Hawkins & Marjorie Hawkins

RULING:
DEFENDANT WISMER & BECKER CONSTRACTING ENGINEERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Wismer & Becker Contracting Engineers' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting the causation element of their claims against defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hawkins (defendant's Exhibits 1, 2, 3; plaintiffs' Exhibit A) precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, that testimony demonstrates that Mr. Hawkins has knowledge that he worked in the presence of defendant's employees when they removed insulation from pipes. Whether defendant's employees worked in the presence of Mr. Hawkins is the sole issue raised by defendant's motion. Defendant's objections to the excerpts of Mr. Hawkins' deposition testimony submitted by plaintiffs are overruled. The Court will not consider defendant's reply evidence. (San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Jennifer Roux of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11033 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.
Dustin M. Seesemann of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Arthur Hawkins & Marjorie Hawkins

RULING:
DEFENDANT MILWAUKEE VALVE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Hawkins was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hawkins and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Mr. Hawkins' deposition testimony demonstrates that he has knowledge that he worked with gaskets and packing supplied by defendant in replacement kits and plaintiffs' discovery responses demonstrate that plaintiffs possess, or can reasonably obtain, evidence that those parts more likely than not contained asbestos. Additionally, plaintiffs' interrogatory responses identify Mr. Hawkins as having knowledge that gaskets and packing he removed from Milwaukee valves were original and defendant failed to demonstrate that he does not have such knowledge. The Court will not consider defendant's reply evidence. (San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Jennifer Roux of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11033 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
